sections.title: A: Use the word given in capitals to form a word
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.
sections.passage: Last summer, my friend Marco went hiking alone in the mountains. He was completely (1) ...... that some wild berries growing along the trail were dangerous. I cannot (2) ...... enough how important it is to research local plants before setting off! Marco ate several of the berries, which meant he urgently needed an (3) ...... of antidote at the nearest clinic, as they contained a highly (4) ...... substance. He began to feel incredibly (5) ...... , with severe cramps and dizziness setting in fast. A (6) ...... at the local hospital confirmed that, while no operation was needed, the case was serious. The (7) ...... Marco received involved close monitoring overnight. Before he was discharged, the doctor gave him a (8) ...... for anti-nausea tablets to take at home. Marco then discovered he was (9) ...... to one of the tablets and developed an itchy rash. Despite this setback, he made a full (10) ...... within ten days and vowed never to eat unknown berries again.

sections.title: B: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:

Your knee ...... recover before the race next weekend.

sections.title: C: Write a phrasal verb in the correct form to replace the words in bold
sections.instructions: Write a phrasal verb in the correct form to replace the words in bold.
